mission.title: Install and Configure Grafana Operator
mission.description: The Grafana Operator simplifies the management of Grafana instances, dashboards, and datasources in Kubernetes. It is ideal for users who prefer to manage their observability stack using Kubernetes-native resources and GitOps workflows. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the Grafana Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Grafana Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Grafana Operator.

```bash

helm repo add grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Grafana Operator Helm chart repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Grafana Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Grafana Operator using Helm, specifying the version to ensure compatibility.

```bash

helm install grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts/grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

```

> Installs the Grafana Operator in the default namespace.

steps.title: Create a Grafana instance
steps.description:
This step creates a custom resource (CR) for Grafana, defining its configuration and settings.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
EOF

```

> Creates a Grafana instance with specified admin credentials.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Grafana instance
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Grafana instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

```

> Displays the details of the Grafana instance.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Grafana Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Grafana Operator from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated Grafana instances and their configurations, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall grafana-operator

kubectl delete grafana grafana

```

> Uninstalls the Grafana Operator.
> Deletes the Grafana instance created earlier.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Grafana Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your Grafana configurations. This step will upgrade the Grafana Operator to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

helm upgrade grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts/grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

```

> Backs up the current Grafana configuration.
> Upgrades the Grafana Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the Grafana instance is still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to recognize "": no matches for kind "Grafana" in version "grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Grafana CRD has not been installed or is not recognized.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Grafana Operator is installed correctly and the CRDs are applied.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install Grafana: Error: unable to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually means that the Kubernetes cluster is not reachable or the kubeconfig is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check your kubeconfig and ensure your Kubernetes cluster is running.

troubleshooting.title: Grafana instance is not starting.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to misconfiguration in the Grafana CR.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Grafana Operator and the Grafana instance for errors using 'kubectl logs'.

troubleshooting.title: Dashboard not loading in Grafana.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This could be due to incorrect JSON configuration in the GrafanaDashboard CR.

**Fix:**
Validate the JSON configuration and ensure it adheres to Grafana's dashboard schema.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Grafana features and improved CRD handling.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Grafana instance running with the status 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get grafana'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
